title: cinchona pubescens var. hirsuta
intro: Cinchona pubescens var. hirsuta, also known as the hairy tree quinine, is a variety of the Cinchona pubescens plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of quinine, a natural alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Cinchona pubescens var. hirsuta include its ability to treat malaria and other fevers, as well as its use as a natural remedy for sore throats, coughs, and colds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, tincture, and powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
